stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of vehicle speed using a speedometer. The measurements have a mean of 163.8 km/h and a standard deviation of 21.3 km/h. However, calibration tests reveal that a speedometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.8% and 9.3%. Estimate the true vehicle speed and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 163.8 km/h. After correcting for the 7.1% bias: ≈ 176.3 km/h. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.8%-9.3%) and sampling variability: [163.6, 189.1]. Point estimate: 176.3 km/h.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
